author: codex-agent

patch.value: Validate UUID fields at telemetry ingestion boundary

Remote telemetry worker logs repeatedly show worker_insert_failed invalid input syntax for type uuid when events contain non-UUID installation_id/session_id/event_id values. Invalid payloads should be rejected (or sanitized/quarantined) before queue insert to prevent retry churn and queue pollution.
Ingestion path validates UUID-typed identifiers before queueing; invalid payloads are dropped/quarantined with explicit counters and do not repeatedly fail worker inserts.

value.author: codex-agent
value.text: Duplicate-check evidence: pm context --limit 10; pm search 'telemetry worker invalid uuid' --limit 10; pm list-open --limit 20; pm list-in-progress --limit 20. Net-new because current telemetry open items cover docs/health visibility but not ingestion boundary UUID validation and worker retry churn.

Outstanding failure signal: telemetry-worker logs repeated worker_insert_failed invalid UUID errors for non-UUID identifiers, indicating ingestion boundary lacks strict UUID validation before worker insert.

This confirms valid events flow while poison messages continue to churn.

value.author: codex-agent
value.text: Current failure mode is queue poison churn: invalid UUID payloads are accepted at ingress and repeatedly fail at worker insert, causing perpetual backlog growth and noisy worker logs.

value.author: codex-agent
value.text: Implementation 2026-04-27: patched remote telemetry-api/app.py to validate and normalize UUID fields before queue publish (event_id, installation_id, optional session_id) and added pm_telemetry_rejected_uuid_events_total field-scoped counter. Patched telemetry-api/worker.py to drop malformed UUID events with ack and explicit worker_drop_invalid_uuid_fields totals instead of nacking/requeueing. Rebuilt telemetry-api/worker images and restarted both services.

value.author: codex-agent
value.text: Verification 2026-04-27 after rollout: queue drained to messages=0/unacked=0, worker logs showed poison backlog drain via worker_drop_invalid_uuid_fields total=6 with no recurring worker_insert_failed UUID churn, invalid UUID POST now returns 400, valid UUID POST returns 200 accepted=1, and /metrics exposes pm_telemetry_rejected_uuid_events_total labels for event_id/installation_id/session_id.

value.author: codex-agent
value.text: Dual boundary hardening is now in place: ingress rejects malformed UUID payloads for new events, while worker ack-drop logic clears any legacy poison messages already in RabbitMQ without requeue loops.

value.author: codex-agent
value.text: When a queue already contains poison telemetry events, API-only validation is insufficient; adding worker-side ack-drop validation prevents indefinite nack/requeue churn and restores queue drain deterministically.

value.author: codex-agent
value.text: Final evidence 2026-04-27: remote smoke checks show telemetry-api healthz ok and queue pm.telemetry.events at 0/0; worker churn no longer loops with worker_insert_failed UUID failures after boundary hardening rollout.

patch.value: Telemetry API now rejects invalid UUID payloads with explicit counters, and the worker drops legacy malformed UUID events without requeue churn.
Invalid UUID telemetry payloads are rejected at ingestion and do not create persistent queue or worker insert failures.
Invalid UUID payloads now return 400 with field-level detail; rejected UUID counters increment; worker drained poison backlog and queue remains at 0/0 without recurring UUID insert-failed loops.
